title: Week 3 Review
html:
Congratulations on completing week three! After finishing your lessons you should now know:

  • All katakana (46 characters) 
  • How to express what you like or you prefer and appraise skill level, how to express reasons, and how to give or receive something, how to express “I understand” using wakarimas (わかります), how to ask about quantities with ikutsu (いくつ) and use counters -mai (-まい) and -dai (-だい)
  • How to use 9 different grammar points:
    • Grammar focus on the particle ga (が) indicating the subject
    • Grammar focus on the adverbs sukoshi (すこし), tada (ただ), yoku (よく), zenzen (ぜんぜん)
    • Grammar focus on the particle doushite (どうして) and kara (から) to explain a reason 
    • Grammar focus on the expression mashou with a verb (Vましょう) to make a proposal
    • Grammar focus on the particle de (で) + Verb to express means
    • Grammar focus on the use of verbs kaeshimas (かえします), agemas (あげます), oshiemas (おしえます)
    • Grammar focus on the particle ni (に) as the recipient of an action
    • Grammar focus on the use of verbs karimas (かります), moraimas (もらいます), naraimas (ならいます)
    • Grammar focus on the difference/or your preference of nouns using the expressions:
      - “Noun1 wa Noun2 yori A des” (N1 は N2 より A です)
      - “Noun1 to Noun2 dochira ga A deska” (N1 と N2 どちら が Aですか)
      - “Noun1 no hou ga A des” (Noun1 の ほう が A です)
If you have any questions on what you have learned this week, don’t forget that you can always ask for more help in the student community!
